reforms.description: The Belgian government introduced a fiscal stimulus package in 2009, which included measures to support companies and enhance employment, specifically focusing on R&D tax reductions for researchers and stimulating growth in young innovative companies through reduced corporate tax rates and a special tax allowance for young innovative SMEs that meet specific R&D spending criteria.
reforms.package_name: R&D Tax Incentives and Support for Innovative SMEs
reforms.component_name: R&D Tax Reductions and Support for SMEs
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The measures are designed to enhance R&D activities and support innovative firms, which are expected to contribute positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures were part of a fiscal stimulus package that was enacted in 2009.
reforms.importance_rationale: The implementation of significant tax incentives for R&D and support for innovative SMEs represents a major reform in Belgium's innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: A number of measures have been taken to stimulate growth and innovation in young innovative companies, who are enjoying reduced corporate tax rates and a special tax allowance for young innovative SMEs.

reforms.description: The government plans to evaluate R&D policies at all levels of government to improve efficiency and effectiveness, suggesting a more centralized approach to R&D governance.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The impact of the evaluation on growth is uncertain as it focuses on governance rather than direct support for R&D activities.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has announced plans for the evaluation but it has not yet been implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The planned evaluation of R&D policies represents a significant step towards improving the governance of innovation policy.
reforms.source_quote: In the area of R&D policies, evaluate R&D policies at all levels of government and also explore where efficiency could be improved by giving more responsibility to the federal government.
